sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Bring real-world payments to Cardano with Wirex, including open-source payments infrastructure, onchain settlement smart contracts, wallet/API ecosystem layer, stablecoin-backed banking rails, compliance architecture, and Visa card issuance linked to onchain balances.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, administered by Intersect for the Wirex project.

Deliverables: WP1 – Enabling Onchain Payments & Card Infrastructure, Visa card issuance linked directly to onchain balances, Onchain settlement through smart contracts, Native integration with self-custody wallets, Wallet and API ecosystem layer, Smart contract engineering, account abstraction, batched transactions, and settlement logic, Milestone-based disbursement via TRSC and PSSC treasury management contracts
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,961,538 ada, composed of 3,846,153 ada for WP1 and 115,385 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that Wirex will deliver full-stack, open-source payments infrastructure for Cardano connecting onchain settlement to banking rails, stablecoin systems, and wallet interfaces.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that a core outcome is Visa card issuance linked directly to onchain balances so users can spend digital assets globally at millions of merchants.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that Wirex has 7 million users, more than 1.5 million cards issued, more than $20 billion in transaction volume, and Visa Principal Member experience.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that the work will create new transaction volume, user growth, institutional participation, and real-world economic activity onchain for Cardano.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and provides voting, documentation, audited-results, and audit-report references.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Wirex will deliver full-stack, open-source payments infrastructure for Cardano connecting onchain settlement to banking rails, stablecoin systems, and wallet interfaces.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that a core outcome is Visa card issuance linked directly to onchain balances so users can spend digital assets globally at millions of merchants.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Wirex has 7 million users, more than 1.5 million cards issued, more than $20 billion in transaction volume, and Visa Principal Member experience.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that the work will create new transaction volume, user growth, institutional participation, and real-world economic activity onchain for Cardano.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,961,538 ada, composed of 3,846,153 ada for WP1 and 115,385 ada for the Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that Wirex will deliver full-stack, open-source payments infrastructure for Cardano connecting onchain settlement to banking rails, stablecoin systems, and wallet interfaces." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
